# Solarferm

> Fuel for the bioeconomy. Solarferm is a feedstock platform for biomanufacturing. Its
> first product is fermentation-grade sugar, produced from carbon dioxide, hydrogen, and
> energy — an abundant, predictable, agriculture-independent feedstock for biomanufacturing
> at industrial scale.

## What Solarferm does
Solarferm is a new feedstock platform for biomanufacturing; its first product is
fermentation-grade sugar, made from carbon dioxide and energy rather than from crops. The
core conversion is thermochemical, combined with engineered biology in a continuous
production loop, turning carbon and energy into programmable sugars (commodity, specialty,
niche, and rare). Output is decoupled from land, weather, harvest, and agricultural
commodity volatility, with consistent quality and predictable pricing.

## Why it matters
Biomanufacturing is becoming national infrastructure, viewed through the lens of food
security, supply chain resilience, and sovereign capability. Its binding constraint is
feedstock supply. Next-generation biomanufacturing needs feedstocks that are abundant,
predictable, cost-effective, and decoupled from agricultural volatility — the constraint
Solarferm exists to remove.

## Who it serves
- Energy companies: carbon and energy monetised as feedstock — a storable, sellable
  product, sited where carbon and energy are abundant or under-utilised, rather than
  competing for contested power; diversifies revenue for the energy transition.
- Biomanufacturers: fermentation-grade sugar with pricing decoupled from crops, delivered
  continuously from modular plants sited near fermentation capacity.
- Ingredient makers: programmable sugars, from commodity to niche and rare, with lower
  carbon intensity than agricultural sources.

## Key points
- Inputs: carbon dioxide (CO₂) and hydrogen (H₂), plus energy.
- Energy-source agnostic: the core conversion is thermochemical, not dependent on cheap
  electricity. Deployed where carbon and energy are abundant or under-utilised, rather
  than competing with rising demand from transport and data centres. Abundant thermal and
  carbon sources, electrification, waste heat, or geothermal.
- Secure, onshore, resilient: supply independent of farms, weather, and distant
  commodity markets.
- Based in New Zealand.

## Contact

- Website: https://solarferm.com/
